stack.rationale: Already in use, provides ISR for storefront, server actions for mutations
Already configured, ensures type safety across codebase
Already in use, utility-first approach matches existing UI components
Already configured in package.json, type-safe SQL, lightweight
Already specified in RFP, serverless-friendly for Next.js
Already in use for cart and checkout stores
Indonesian payment gateway, required by client
Client's chosen provider for transactional emails
Client's chosen provider for product images, integrates seamlessly with Vercel deployment
Already in use, accessible headless components
Already configured, schema validation with type inference
jose already in dependencies, JWT-based session management

architectureNotes.heading: Vertical Slice Architecture
architectureNotes.body: SPEC.md prescribes vertical slice architecture where each feature is self-contained. Frontend already follows this pattern. Backend should maintain same structure with domain/application/presentation layers only when complexity warrants it. Simple CRUD features can skip domain layer.

architectureNotes.heading: No API routes exist currently
architectureNotes.body: All backend work is greenfield. The app/(store) and app/admin directories have complete UI but no app/api directory. Need to create all API routes from scratch. Consider using Next.js server actions for mutations (per SPEC.md) and route handlers for queries.

architectureNotes.heading: Database schema required
architectureNotes.body: No lib/db directory exists. SPEC.md documents schema but file not created. Tables needed: users, customers, addresses, categories, brands, products, product_variants, orders, order_items, payments, promotions, banners, verification_tokens. Drizzle migrations need to be generated and run against Neon.

architectureNotes.heading: ISR for Storefront, CSR for Admin
architectureNotes.body: Spec calls for Incremental Static Regeneration for storefront pages (products, categories) for performance. Admin dashboard uses client-side rendering with TanStack Query for real-time data. This split architecture is appropriate for the use case.

architectureNotes.heading: Session management approach
architectureNotes.body: jose library is in dependencies suggesting JWT-based sessions. Store session token in HTTP-only cookie. Session should contain user ID and role for RBAC. Neon serverless is stateless so JWT is appropriate over database sessions.

architectureNotes.heading: Guest checkout with auto-registration
architectureNotes.body: Users can checkout without prior registration. During checkout, contact data (name, email, phone) is collected. Upon successful order completion, a customer account is automatically created using this data. The new customer receives login credentials via email. This reduces friction while still building a customer database.

architectureNotes.heading: Simple availability model (no stock quantities)
architectureNotes.body: Products have an availability flag (available/unavailable) instead of stock quantity tracking. This simplifies the system - no stock deductions, no low-stock alerts, no stock returns on cancellation. Admin manually toggles availability. Suitable for a dive shop where inventory management is handled externally or not needed initially.

architectureNotes.heading: No reviews/ratings in initial scope
architectureNotes.body: Product reviews and ratings are deferred to a future phase. Simplifies product detail pages and removes need for reviews table, moderation workflow, and rating aggregation logic.

risks.text: Midtrans webhook in sandbox may not match production behavior perfectly - test thoroughly
ISR revalidation timing may cause stale product data immediately after admin updates
Email deliverability to Indonesian ISPs via Mailgun may have issues - verify Mailgun has good regional delivery rates
Zustand cart state needs careful sync with server-side cart to avoid client-server mismatch
Drizzle ORM with Neon serverless may have connection pooling issues under load - verify connection management
Auto-registration may create duplicate accounts if guest uses existing email - need email lookup and account linking logic
